scope: sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/src non-native production TypeScript from llmswitch-rustification-audit

classificationContract.native_shell_ok: TypeScript is a fail-fast native facade or type projection over Rust truth.
classificationContract.type_shell_ok: TypeScript is compile-time/public API shape only and owns no runtime decision.
classificationContract.ts_io_shell_ok: TypeScript owns unavoidable Node host IO, timers, filesystem, process, Map storage, or diagnostics emission only.
classificationContract.parser_io_ok: TypeScript parses native JSON strings and type-projects Rust-owned output only.
classificationContract.diagnostic_io_ok: TypeScript records or emits diagnostic observations only; no request/response/route/tool policy.
classificationContract.hardStopRule: Any file not listed here, or any listed file with an empty cannotShrinkFurtherBecause reason, is rustification debt.

entries.minimumTsRole: StageRecorder interface for TS tests and package type surface.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Pure type surface has no Rust runtime behavior to migrate; deleting it breaks current TypeScript test/package imports before a separate generated-type replacement exists.

entries.minimumTsRole: Provider response Node IO shell that reads streams, invokes Rust Hub Pipeline/effect planners, executes host side effects, and writes returned SSE frames.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: The remaining code touches Node Readable streams, MetadataCenter host objects, response store IO, usage persistence, servertool shell execution, stage recording, and returned SSE stream construction. Provider response parsing, governance, projection, effect planning, and error descriptors are Rust-owned through native calls.

entries.minimumTsRole: Type declarations for TS host/Rust bridge boundaries.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Current Node package compiles TS callers against these structural types; further removal requires a separate Rust-generated declaration pipeline, not Hub runtime migration.

entries.minimumTsRole: In-process timing observation state for Node diagnostics.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: The state is tied to Node process diagnostic logging and MetadataCenter debug snapshots; migrating it means a separate diagnostics/runtime IO rewrite, not Hub semantic rustification.

entries.minimumTsRole: Node-local diagnostic timing aggregation state for hub-stage top-summary reporting.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Only Node-local diagnostic state remains; there is no Hub/VR runtime semantic decision to migrate without rewriting the diagnostics sink.

entries.minimumTsRole: Reads environment flags for diagnostic timing only.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Environment access is Node host IO; moving it to Rust would not reduce Hub semantics and needs a separate process-config migration.

entries.minimumTsRole: Formats and emits diagnostic timing log lines.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Console/log emission is Node diagnostics IO; Rust owns Hub semantics already, and further removal needs a diagnostics sink rewrite.

entries.minimumTsRole: Node-local diagnostic timing state used by console and debug snapshot emission.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: This is process-local observation state; deleting it removes current diagnostics and migrating it is a separate observability runtime project.

entries.minimumTsRole: Node-local timing timeline state used only by hub-stage diagnostic observation.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Only diagnostic timeline bookkeeping remains; there is no Rust semantic owner gap to close.

entries.minimumTsRole: Attaches timing summaries to MetadataCenter debug snapshots and logs observations.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: It must touch JS MetadataCenter/debug snapshot objects and console IO in the current Node host; moving it requires replacing the diagnostics host surface.

entries.minimumTsRole: TS structural contract for host/native request and adapter context wiring.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Current TS host modules compile against these public shapes; Rust cannot remove compile-time TS declarations without a separate generated bindings system.

entries.minimumTsRole: Shared JSON structural type and clone helper for TS-only IO/tests.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: It is a TypeScript compile-time/public utility for JS JSON values; Rust migration cannot replace TS type checking without generated declaration adoption.

entries.minimumTsRole: Host-side standardized request/response structural declarations.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: These are TS compiler contracts for the Node host boundary; Rust owns runtime builders, but TS declarations remain until generated bindings replace them.

entries.minimumTsRole: Responses bridge TS host type declarations.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Only compile-time shape declarations remain; further removal requires generated Rust/NAPI declaration replacement.

entries.minimumTsRole: Node Map/FS/global singleton/timer/error-projection shell executing Rust native plans.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: The remaining code owns Node-only persistence files, process-global singleton state, timers, Map indexes, and exception projection. Moving it further requires a separate Rust-backed persistent store plus Node host lifecycle replacement; no Hub continuation semantics remain in TS.

entries.minimumTsRole: Legacy conversion package type declarations.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: This is public TS type/API surface only; deletion needs package API migration, not Rust runtime work.

entries.minimumTsRole: Bridge message TS declarations for host compile boundary.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Current bridge host code and tests compile against these TS declarations; only generated bindings or full TS bridge removal can eliminate them.

entries.minimumTsRole: Fail-fast native policy facade for provider failure decisions.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Node executor/provider shells call this facade; removal requires moving those host call sites or replacing them with generated bindings.

entries.minimumTsRole: Virtual Router public TS config/contract declaration surface.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: VR runtime is Rust-only, but Node config/bootstrap/tests compile against these TS contract declarations. Removing them requires generated Rust schema declarations or a full config API migration.

entries.minimumTsRole: Node filesystem/path environment resolver.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: It is host OS/path IO; moving it requires a separate runtime lifecycle Rust migration and does not close Hub/VR semantics.

entries.minimumTsRole: Servertool progress-file filesystem IO for Node-hosted diagnostic/progress artifacts.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Remaining behavior is file IO in the Node host. Rust owns servertool semantics; further shrink needs a separate file-output runtime migration.

entries.minimumTsRole: Servertool TS host interface declarations.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Current TS servertool shells/tests compile against these types; Rust owns execution semantics but cannot eliminate TypeScript declarations without generated bindings or full shell removal.

entries.minimumTsRole: Generic JS object/error formatting helpers used by TS IO shells.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Helpers operate on JS unknown/object values at the host boundary; moving them only makes sense after the TS IO shells they support are removed.

entries.minimumTsRole: Node telemetry aggregation sink for Rust-projected events.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Rust projects VR hit-log events; this file owns the JS process telemetry sink. Removing it requires a separate telemetry runtime replacement.
